title: Eva 13 Entry Plug Seat Directions Split
display_title: Eva 13 Entry Plug Seat Directions Split
wikitext:
[]: Eva-13 Entry Plug Seat Directions [Split]             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Tue Oct 08, 2013 2:14 am [Split from "Eva-13 and Shinji -- who was really in control?" . - OMF] One question I have about the position of the pilot seats in EVA-13. Are they inverted? The scene with the dummy plug being installed in Unit-01 shows the seat faces away from the ejection jets, but in EVA-13 case or Shinji's entry plug, his seat is facing the jets. It would also explain why Shinji and Kaworu are facing away at an angle instead of having their seats facing towards each other when watching their entry plugs being inserted towards each other in EVA-13. If I've made a mistake (I still haven't got the damn movie yet) or someone has already pointed this out please correct. Remember, in this movie, every little detail is telling a story or pointing us somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Mon Oct 14, 2013 4:21 am Point of interest: EVA-13's entry plugs have reversed seats. ElMariachi wrote: They aren't, from where do you get that idea?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Oct 14, 2013 5:40 am The entry plug seat always faces the core, for Shinji's and Kaworu's visuals, take in account that it's not the walls who becomes transparent, but just a camera showing the co-pilot's cockpit for better communication, so off course the camera will them side-to-side, while their actually in a V formation facing the core from two different sides. And the entry plug have ejection jets of both sides, and the side facing the exterior(the side the seat is facing away) have a parachute that activates soon after ejecting. To understand it better, you can look at the artwork(thanks to SEELE-01 for finding that little gem) : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Mon Oct 14, 2013 6:58 am ElMariachi wrote: The entry plug seat always faces the core, for Shinji's and Kaworu's visuals, take in account that it's not the walls who becomes transparent, but just a camera showing the co-pilot's cockpit for better communication, so off course the camera will them side-to-side, while their actually in a V formation facing the core from two different sides. And the entry plug have ejection jets of both sides, and the side facing the exterior(the side the seat is facing away) have a parachute that activates soon after ejecting. To understand it better, you can look at the artwork(thanks to SEELE-01 for finding that little gem) : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Oct 14, 2013 8:18 am wiser3754 wrote: When I watched the entry plugs being installed in a V formation on the back of EVA-13, the entry plugs are obviously pointing towards each other, however when the visuals are activated, Kaworu and Shinji are seen pointing away from each other, not towards. That could only happen if their seats are facing upwards or in reverse.

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Mon Oct 14, 2013 2:30 pm monitoradiation wrote: The plugs aren't installed in reverse, per se. The display wall around them are shifted projections for the convenience of the pilots' visualization. It doesn't matter how they're installed, as long as the mapping of the pilots' actions translate to the same movements in the Eva. ElMariachi is correct - The lightning-pattern appears at both ends of the entry plugs on Eva-13. You see this when the plugs are being installed.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Oct 14, 2013 3:14 pm wiser3754 wrote: I'm talking about the seats in EVA-13's entry plugs being reversed, not the entry plug being installed in reverse. Also, the lightning pattern (ejection jets) only appear on one side of the plugs, the other side contains the parachute ports. And I know that the entry plug at the end of 3.33 is the ejection end sticking up, not the parachute, there's no parachute cords connected to it, and again, Shinji's seat is facing the ejection end, not the parachute front, meaning that the design of the seat was meant to be in reverse, facing away from the EVA core.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Oct 14, 2013 3:36 pm Also notice that when Asuka is walking toward Shinji to kick him, she isn't struggling as if she's on an ascending incline, that's supported by the fact that when she pushes Shinji, he doesn't fall backward, toward Asuka and the seat, but forward, which wouldn't be possible if the seat was facing the sticking out ejection side of the entry plug.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Oct 14, 2013 3:42 pm ElMariachi wrote: Also notice that when Asuka is walking toward Shinji to kick him, she isn't struggling as if she's on an ascending incline, that's supported by the fact that when she pushes Shinji, he doesn't fall backward, toward Asuka and the seat, but forward, which wouldn't be possible if the seat was facing the sticking out ejection side of the entry plug.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Oct 14, 2013 4:23 pm Wait : That's the side facing the core(the jets are at the white "notches" at the extremity) : That's the eject side, with the jets clearly labeled in yellow : Asuka facing Shinji and the seat(the metallic thing at the upper right : Her position on top of the entry plug, the eject side behind her : Holy fuck, wiser3754 is right, the seat is facing away from the core! Is it a necessary particularity for the Evangelions based on an ADAM and it was the same thing for Mark.06 and Mark.09, or was there another reason(preventing Shinji to reach a too high plug depth, being absorbed and thus the control system of EVA-13?), or just an animation error. Guess we will have to wait for the 3.0 CRC, if EVA-13's entry plug has the seat reversed it will surely be shown in it.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Oct 14, 2013 4:48 pm ElMariachi wrote: Snip

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Oct 14, 2013 4:57 pm monitoradiation wrote: 2. That's the only shot in which Asuka is indicated to be the high side of the plug. Right after that shot, she turns AROUND to walk off of the plug, it makes no sense to head higher if she's going to jump off immediately. Then after she comes back, she jumps in the plug behind Shinji, hops onto the front of the pilot seat, then kicks Shinji - as you've mentioned, he falls forwards, not backwards. I'm still leaning towards animation error due to this being hinged mostly on that one shot of Asuka appearing at the high side of the plug. More likely, I think this is a case of overlooked shot mismangement. The external shot was probably supposed to be used after Asuka came back right before she jumps in the plug behind Shinji. They decided to show it right after she opened the hatch but forgot to change her location.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Oct 14, 2013 5:00 pm ElMariachi wrote: But then if the seat is facing away the ejection side, why would Asuka take the hassle to climb all the way up to jump inside the entry plug instead of entering from the lower side?(the purple one) Was it just to land precisely behind Shinji to don't have to circle him to kick him in the back? At least that would mean that Asuka kept her taste of making a perfect entrance!

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Tue Oct 15, 2013 4:58 am ElMariachi wrote: Wait : That's the side facing the core(the jets are at the white "notches" at the extremity) : That's the eject side, with the jets clearly labeled in yellow : Asuka facing Shinji and the seat(the metallic thing at the upper right : Her position on top of the entry plug, the eject side behind her : Holy fuck, wiser3754 is right, the seat is facing away from the core! Is it a necessary particularity for the Evangelions based on an ADAM and it was the same thing for Mark.06 and Mark.09, or was there another reason(preventing Shinji to reach a too high plug depth, being absorbed and thus the control system of EVA-13?), or just an animation error. Guess we will have to wait for the 3.0 CRC, if EVA-13's entry plug has the seat reversed it will surely be shown in it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Oct 15, 2013 5:25 am The problem is that there is still things that doesn't add up : - as monitoradiation pointed out, on the third image you quoted we see Asuka's feet on the purple side of the hatch, which is on the lower side of the opened hatch, yet when we see a wide shot of the plug she's standing on the gray side of the hatch! - even stranger : going back to the artwork of the entry plug I posted on the previous page, the mechanism of the explosive bolts that Asuka uses is depicted on the upper-right... and the button to trigger the bolts are on the purple side of the main door, opposite to where Asuka is shown standing on the wide shot! And when the hatch opened, we saw Asuka leaning on the opening, then making a face and straightening up before lecturing Shinji, she didn't moved at all... yet she's shown in the wide shot as being on the opposite side of the hatch where the trigger button is supposed to be! I think we may have found the first non-euclidean entry plug in history! More seriously, there is definitively an animation error somewhere in that scene. wiser3754 wrote: One last thing, when Asuka opens the hatch, air escapes, indicating pressure. Could Shinji have suffocated in the plug if he just sat there?

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Tue Oct 15, 2013 6:01 am ElMariachi wrote: The problem is that there is still things that doesn't add up : - as monitoradiation pointed out, on the third image you quoted we see Asuka's feet on the purple side of the hatch, which is on the lower side of the opened hatch, yet when we see a wide shot of the plug she's standing on the gray side of the hatch! - even stranger : going back to the artwork of the entry plug I posted on the previous page, the mechanism of the explosive bolts that Asuka uses is depicted on the upper-right... and the button to trigger the bolts are on the purple side of the main door, opposite to where Asuka is shown standing on the wide shot! And when the hatch opened, we saw Asuka leaning on the opening, then making a face and straightening up before lecturing Shinji, she didn't moved at all... yet she's shown in the wide shot as being on the opposite side of the hatch where the trigger button is supposed to be! I think we may have found the first non-euclidean entry plug in history! More seriously, there is definitively an animation error somewhere in that scene. Since the entry plug are hermetically sealed when they close(to prevent LCL from leaking away), then yes, had Asuka not found him, Shinji would probably have ended up dying of suffocation, not that he minds it in his current state...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Oct 15, 2013 11:52 am wiser3754 wrote: Isn't the lighting from the core material reflecting onto the gray steel giving off a purplish hue at the bottom right and a reddish hue from the top left?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Oct 15, 2013 1:04 pm ElMariachi wrote: Everything points that Asuka is on the lower side of the entry plug, yet on the wide shot she's shown to be on the upper side...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 15, 2013 1:11 pm Dude, you can't even see the full seat in those shots anyway. It matters less than Shinii's magical plug suit in Episode 20 of NGE, which engulfed the whole frame.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jul 19, 2014 8:24 pm I've split this topic off from its original for a more focused discussion. While this does look like an animation goof, you can't be too careful when it comes to Eva minutae. We know Shinji's Plug (Llittle alpha) is inserted on the Eva's left hand side, While Kaworu's (Big Alpha) plug is inserted on the right. [wkimg]File:Eva3-33 C0938 dual-entry.jpg[/wkimg] Inside the cockpit, Shinji and Kaworu are indeed on the left and right respectviely, but their plug orientations are completely askew. [wkimg]File:Eva3.33-C0942_Eva-13_Dual_cockpit.jpg[/wkimg] As has already been noted, this view is a projection of the Entry plug's visual systems, and so can't necessarily be viewed as an actual image of the Plug's positions. However, one important point to note is that the standout feature of the D-type system is the "musical score" connection complex which sits behind the pilots in their respective cockpits. Again this is all a virtual interface so the absolute position of this interface is not certain. But if it's presence is meant to suggest that the connection interface between the pilots is situated on the seat behind them, then in order to "enhance" any connection by increasing plug depth the seats would have to be facing backwards. That's about the only reason I can think of for why there would be an alternative seating direction for the pilots.
